name: airprowl
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``airprowl`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: cargo build --release then ./target/release/airprowl (TUI); sudo ./target/release/airprowl for monitor-mode client sniffing + Sub-GHz; airprowl --scan bt|wifi|subghz for headless JSON to stdout

examples.command: cargo build --release then ./target/release/airprowl (TUI); sudo ./target/release/airprowl for monitor-mode client sniffing + Sub-GHz; airprowl --scan bt|wifi|subghz for headless JSON to stdout

algorithms: WiFi AP scanning via periodic nmcli dev wifi list polling with escaped-colon parser + /sys operstate health check
Passive WiFi client sniffing via iw monitor interface + pnet raw datalink capture + libwifi 802.11 frame parse (probe requests, data, QoS)
EMA signal smoothing (alpha=0.4) with trend-arrow snapshots
Log-distance path-loss distance estimation with per-source reference RSSI/exponent
OUI vendor lookup against embedded 49K-entry IEEE OUI database (nmap-mac-prefixes)
Bluetooth SIG Company ID lookup (3971 entries) + BlueZ icon device-class + LAA/RPA random-address detection
BlueZ D-Bus BLE/BR-EDR discovery with manufacturer_data + tx_power
Sub-GHz CC1111 USB bulk control: register-level FREQ set + RSSI read across 315/433/868/915 MHz ISM bands
Custom device-name persistence keyed by MAC/frequency (names.json)
produces: JSON device exports (e key or --scan mode): BT records (address, name, vendor, icon, company_id, is_random, rssi_dbm, rssi_avg_dbm, tx_power, distance_m, samples, seen_secs_ago, tracked_secs, custom_name)
WiFi records (mac, kind ap/client, ssid, vendor, probing_for, signal_pct, rssi_dbm, channel, frequency, security, distance_m, samples, custom_name)
Sub-GHz records (freq_hz, freq_mhz, band, rssi_dbm, rssi_avg_dbm, samples, custom_name)
Top-level scan metadata (scan_type, duration_secs, device_count, status/diagnostic warnings)
Live TUI: sortable per-band tables with color-coded EMA-smoothed RSSI, trend arrows, distance estimates, stale/drop lifecycle
novelty: Genuinely novel, not an airodump-ng wrapper. WiFi AP scanning shells out to nmcli (wrapper-ish), but WiFi client sniffing is native Rust (pnet + libwifi + custom radiotap RSSI), not airodump-ng/hcxtools. Differentiators vs baseline: (1) unified tri-band BT+WiFi+Sub-GHz TUI — no baseline tool covers Sub-GHz; (2) direct USB register-level CC1111 Sub-GHz RSSI across 4 ISM bands is absent from airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy entirely; (3) EMA smoothing + trend arrows + log-distance path-loss distance estimation surfaced live (airodump-ng shows raw RSSI only); (4) embedded compile-time OUI (49K) + Bluetooth SIG company DB (3971) with no runtime file/network dependency; (5) RPA/LAA random-address detection; (6) persistent custom device naming (names.json); (7) single ~4MB async Rust binary with monitor-iface Drop teardown. Does NOT implement PMKID/handshake/EAPOL/WPS/deauth — it is a passive enumeration/tracking tool, not a credential harvester.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
